design_summary.purpose: Provide an 8-bit parameterized PWM generator that counts on a single synchronous clock, wraps at a programmable period boundary, and asserts PWM output while the live counter value is below the programmed duty threshold.
design_summary.operating_model: The design operates in one synchronous clock domain on clk with active-low synchronous reset_n. When reset_n is low, sequential state clears to zero. When reset_n is high and enable is asserted, counter_value advances on each rising edge of clk; if the enabled count reaches the programmed period value, the next enabled state returns to zero. pwm_out is derived from the current counter_value versus duty_cycle comparison and is exposed alongside the counter state as a continuously available output.

module_architecture.name: pwm_controller
module_architecture.role: Single-module RTL implementation of the PWM controller described by the spec.
responsibilities: Maintain a synchronous 8-bit counter with enable gating.
Implement wrap-to-zero behavior at the configured period boundary.
Generate pwm_out as a comparison of counter_value against duty_cycle using the current counter state.
Ensure all sequential state returns to zero under active-low reset_n.
Remain synthesizable for standard ASIC synthesis and STA flows with no inferred latches, no tri-states, and no unsized arithmetic.
interface_notes: clk is the sole clocking reference for all sequential behavior.
reset_n is active-low and clears sequential state synchronously according to the specified operating model.
enable controls whether the counter holds or advances on a given clock edge.
duty_cycle and period are treated as live 8-bit unsigned inputs sampled each cycle.
pwm_out and counter_value are the only driven outputs.
Input widths are fixed as specified and are not to be redefined.
ownership_notes: Counter state ownership remains internal to pwm_controller and is exposed through counter_value.
Output generation ownership remains internal to pwm_controller and is derived from the live comparison of counter_value and duty_cycle.
No additional modules, submodules, or hierarchical decomposition are implied by the spec.

data_flow_summary: clk and reset_n control all sequential updates within pwm_controller.
enable qualifies counter progression; when low, counter_value holds its current value.
period defines the wrap threshold for the enabled counter path.
duty_cycle defines the comparison threshold that determines pwm_out.
counter_value is driven as the current counter state and is the basis for pwm_out generation.
pwm_out reflects whether counter_value is strictly less than duty_cycle under normal operation.

clock_reset_summary.clocking: Single synchronous clock domain on clk at 50.0 MHz with a 20.0 ns period.
clock_reset_summary.reset_behavior: reset_n is active low and synchronous; when asserted low, all sequential state is forced to zero. Upon release of reset_n, counter_value restarts from zero and pwm_out follows normal comparison behavior using the current counter state and duty_cycle.

integration_notes: period and duty_cycle are live inputs and are not shadowed by hidden registers per the fixed assumptions.
The design is intended for direct integration into standard ASIC RTL, synthesis, and STA flows.
The RTL output filename is fixed by the spec as pwm_controller.v and must not be altered.
Power intent, timing constraints, and handoff deliverables are expected to align with this single-module implementation without introducing new hierarchy.
consistency_notes: No hierarchy, ports, or filenames may differ from digital_spec_json.
